question_text: Using vector analysis, prove that the moment of a force F in 3D space, with respect to an arbitrary axis defined in the Cartesian coordinate system, remains constant regardless of the point chosen on the arbitrary axis.
question_latex: \text{Using vector analysis, prove that the moment of a force } \mathbf{F} \text{ in 3D space, with respect to an arbitrary axis defined in the Cartesian coordinate system, remains constant regardless of the point chosen on the arbitrary axis.}
subject: Basics of Mechanical Engineering

question_text: Discuss the transformation of a determinate truss into an indeterminate one, accompanied by mathematical reasoning. Additionally, illustrate the process with appropriate diagrams.
question_latex: \text{Discuss the transformation of a determinate truss into an indeterminate one, accompanied by mathematical reasoning.}
subject: Basics of Mechanical Engineering

question_text: For a simple screw jack, derive the expressions for effort applied at the circumference of spindle and force applied at the end of the lever, when the load is hoisted upwards.
question_latex: \text{For a simple screw jack, derive the expressions for effort applied at the circumference of spindle (P) and force applied at the end of the lever (P}_1\text{) when the load (W) is hoisted upwards.}
subject: Basics of Mechanical Engineering
